title: Adopt PR+CI gate for epic integration via .tick/config.md
description:
Proposed in the kgh (Execution Model v2 Phase 4) retro as "PR+CI gate adoption via .tick/config.md once repo has CI wired". The precondition is now met: .github/workflows/ci.yml exists (gates UI typecheck and runs the cloud/worker suite, among others).

Context: the orchestrator reads .tick/config.md at run start (skills/ticks/references/claude-runner.md, "Run-start: reading .tick/config.md"). It supports three sections — Testing (exact test commands passed to implementers), Environment (pre-flight checks run once before wave 1), Rules (project-specific constraints included verbatim in every implementer prompt). This repo has no .tick/config.md yet.

Work — create .tick/config.md for this repo:
- Testing: go test ./... ; UI: pnpm install + pnpm test in internal/tickboard/ui ; worker: pnpm install + tests in cloud/worker (pnpm, never npm/yarn, per CLAUDE.md).
- Environment: verify go, pnpm, and git are on PATH.
- Rules: epic integration goes through a PR + CI gate — the orchestrator pushes the epic branch, opens a PR, and the epic close-out may not complete until ci.yml is green on that PR; no direct merges of epic branches to the default branch. Keep wording concise — Rules are injected verbatim into every implementer prompt.

This tick is gated --requires approval because it adopts project policy: the human reviews the drafted config.md (especially the Rules wording) before it takes effect.

Files likely touched: .tick/config.md (new).
Verification: file parses as the three documented sections; a dry read-through against claude-runner.md run-start procedure shows each section is actionable.
acceptance_criteria: .tick/config.md exists with Testing, Environment, and Rules sections; Rules require epic branches to merge via PR with ci.yml green before epic close; human approval recorded on this tick
